column1: # proofgate default rules
# Columns (TAB-separated): id, tool, scope, pattern, action, reason
#   tool:    bash | edit | any
#   scope:   regex matched against cwd; * = everywhere
#   pattern: regex anchored at the start of each command head (bash) or
#            searched in file_path/content (edit). Optional guard prefix:
#              outside-cwd-tmp:  fires only if a path argument resolves outside cwd and temp dirs
#              dirty-repo:       fires only if the cwd git repo has uncommitted changes
#   action:  deny | ask | require-token (single-use token minted by bin/pg-grant)
#   reason:  shown to the model; {head} expands to the matched command head
# User overlay: $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA/rules.local.tsv (a row with the same id replaces a default).
rm-rf-outside-cwd
column6: Recursive force-delete targets paths outside the working directory and temp dirs: {head}. Re-scope the deletion to the project tree or /tmp, or ask the user to run it themselves.

column1: git-discard-dirty
column6: {head} permanently discards uncommitted changes in a dirty repo.

column1: git-push-force
column6: Force push rewrites remote history. Use --force-with-lease instead (it refuses to overwrite commits you have not seen): git push --force-with-lease <remote> <branch>.

column1: git-commit-no-verify
column6: git commit --no-verify skips this repo's commit hooks. Confirm with the user before bypassing checks.

column1: chmod-777
column6: {head} makes the whole tree world-writable. Confirm with the user; prefer the narrowest mode that works.

column1: # --- Example custom rules: copy into $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA/rules.local.tsv and uncomment ---
# Repo-scoped: merges in repos matching 'my-app' land only via the merge bot.
# merge-via-bot
column6: Merges in my-app land via the merge bot; comment '/merge' on the PR instead of merging directly.

column1: # Terminal-workspace tools (e.g. cmux): closing a workspace kills its running processes.
# workspace-close
column6: Closing a terminal workspace (e.g. workspace-7) kills everything running in it: {head}. Confirm with the user first.
